findings.title: Google Fonts loaded via external @import — no new npm package
findings.detail: Nunito is loaded via a Google Fonts CDN @import in globals.css. This introduces a render-blocking external request and a third-party font dependency. No npm package is added, so no CVE or licence risk. However, for production the Next.js font optimisation API (next/font/google) would eliminate the render-blocking request, self-host the font, and improve LCP.
findings.fix: Consider replacing the CSS @import with next/font/google in layout.tsx: import { Nunito } from 'next/font/google'; const nunito = Nunito({ weight: ['700','800','900'], subsets: ['latin'], variable: '--font-nunito' }); — then apply nunito.variable to <html>. This self-hosts the font and removes the external CDN dependency.

summary: No new npm packages introduced, no CVEs, no licence issues; one suggestion to replace CDN-based Google Fonts import with next/font/google for LCP improvement.
